raw_consumer_statement:
My online XXXX account was hacked on XX/XX/year>. The hacker used my account to make purchases with my credit card on file ( Barclays JetBlue ) and picked up the item in person and XXXX dispensed the item because they dont check ID for online pick up orders. I immediately received notification from my card about suspicious activity and the hacker was prevented from making an additional purchase. One of the purchases was deemed fraudulent by Barclays but the other was not despite the fact that these purchases were made within an hour of each other and were the exact same item ( airpods ) but just picked up at different store locations. This makes absolutely no sense whatsoever and yet Barclay refuses to erase the second charge that this hacker made. 
I've provided information about what happened multiple times to the company, filed a police report and received terrible service, bad agents on the phone and conflicting information every time I talk to someone. The office of the president responded to my previous post here but they never respond to emails and the phone number provided never works ( it's essentially a fake number that never puts you through, doesn't give you any options and is likely just a way to frustrate you enough to get you to go away ). 

Essentially they just try to freeze you out of trying to ever get ahold of anyone, then they make a decision against you and hope you'll just give up. Sorry but this frankly criminal enterpise needs to be held to account. I've closed all my accounts with this horrible company but ultimately I still want my {$160.00} back. Like genuinely never encountered a company that was SO greedy they would rather lose a customer with 10s of thousands of dollars in various accounts with your bank than just give back {$160.00} of fraudulent charges.
